title: Portfolio Management
description: Construction of optimal portfolios using Markowitz theory (Mean-Variance) and Sharpe ratio maximization. Efficient frontier analysis and asset allocation.
longDescription: This project implements the Markowitz Mean-Variance optimization framework to construct efficient investment portfolios. It calculates the expected returns, covariance matrix, and the efficient frontier. The model allows for the identification of the Global Minimum Variance (GMV) portfolio and the Tangency Portfolio (Maximum Sharpe Ratio). Key features include visualization of the risk-return tradeoff and dynamic asset allocation strategies based on historical data.

title: Systemic Risk & CoVaR Modeling of US Mega-Caps
description: Quantitative assessment of systemic risk contribution for four major US stocks across key sectors. The analysis estimates dynamic Value at Risk (VaR) and $\Delta$CoVaR using Quantile Regression, Parametric GJR-GARCH, and Asymmetric CaViaR models.
longDescription: Comprehensive quantitative analysis evaluating the systemic risk contribution of four major corporations (JPMorgan, Progressive, NVIDIA, and NextEra Energy) relative to the S&P 500 benchmark. The study compares Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) with Quantile Regression to analyze tail-risk dependencies and changing correlation structures during extreme market conditions. Furthermore, it implements advanced VaR estimation techniques, comparing Parametric GJR-GARCH with t-Student distributions against Asymmetric CaViaR models, to construct dynamic CoVaR and $\Delta$CoVaR metrics. Key findings reveal that the utility sector representative (NextEra Energy) exhibited the highest systemic risk contribution, a contagion effect severely amplified during the COVID-19 crisis. Conversely, the technology representative (NVIDIA) displayed predominantly idiosyncratic risk despite having the highest individual volatility.

title: Derivatives Valuation
description: Options valuation using the Black-Scholes model and Monte Carlo simulation. Sensitivity analysis through the Greeks (Delta, Gamma, Vega, Theta).
longDescription: A comprehensive tool for pricing financial derivatives. It implements the closed-form Black-Scholes-Merton model for European options and Monte Carlo simulation for path-dependent options. The project emphasizes 'The Greeks'—Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega, and Rho—to perform sensitivity analysis and manage portfolio risk. It also features implied volatility calculations using the Newton-Raphson numerical method.

title: Time Series Analysis
description: Estimation of ARIMA and VAR models for financial time series forecasting. Stationarity diagnostics, cointegration, and impulse-response analysis.
longDescription: This econometric project explores advanced time series modeling for financial forecasting. It covers the complete workflow: from stationarity testing (ADF, KPSS) and seasonality adjustment to the estimation of ARIMA (for univariate) and VAR (for multivariate) models. The analysis includes Granger causality tests, impulse-response functions (IRF), and variance decomposition to understand the dynamic relationships between economic variables.

title: Interest Rate Derivatives Pricing & Volatility Modeling
description: Valuation of collateralized Interest Rate Swaps (IRS) and Caps, including implied volatility estimation using advanced market models.
longDescription: Comprehensive quantitative project focused on the valuation of collateralized fixed-income derivatives in EUR. The scope includes the detailed pricing of a 20-year Fixed/Floating Interest Rate Swap (IRS) and an Interest Rate Cap tied to the Euribor-6M, strictly applying real-world market conventions (such as Modified Following, Target calendars, and specific day count fractions like Act/360 and 30/360). Additionally, the project involves calculating the equivalent implied volatility for the Cap structure utilizing a Shifted Log-normal (3%) model.
